lines: A stone set low in the wall of the westmost cell lifts free on a fingernail. Behind it, dry where nothing else is, sits a flat tin the size of a prayer book.

Inside: a heavy iron key, oiled against rust, and a scrap folded small. 'For the wall box. No pay in it worth dying for. I left it for whoever outlives this place, not for the diocese. They can dig their own holes.'
You pocket the key. Old iron sits cold and good in the hand.
